untranslatable_reason: Signal requires explicit trend line detection and breakout confirmation against those lines. The allowed indicators do not include trend line identification (would need slope/angle calculation or historical line tracking). 'Touch bottom trend line and move up' and 'touch top trend line and move down' require dynamic trend line construction not supported by the available indicator set (Donchian channels are closest but not equivalent to user-drawn trend lines). The partial rise/decline as breakout signal also requires pattern-specific state tracking beyond basic indicators.
translation_notes: This is a classic trend line breakout pattern that requires identifying user-defined trend lines and detecting when price touches and breaks through them. The available indicators (moving averages, Donchian channels, support/resistance pivots) approximate trend behavior but cannot directly identify arbitrary trend lines or measure distance from them. A production implementation would need custom trend line detection logic or require traders to supply trend line price levels as external parameters.
